reasoning: The return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia to the US before July 1, 2025 (a window of just two months from the May 1, 2025 knowledge cutoff) involves overcoming immense political and international barriers. While US Federal Courts and the Supreme Court ordered the Trump administration to "facilitate" his return after an erroneous deportation, the Executive Branch vehemently opposes it. Both President Trump and El Salvador's President Bukele have publicly stated they will not return him to the US. Currently, Abrego Garcia is locked in a maximum-security prison in El Salvador. Even as a federal judge threatens contempt, the administration can likely satisfy the judicial standard of "facilitating" return by formally asking El Salvador, which Bukele would summarily reject, leaving US courts with no jurisdiction to compel a foreign sovereign nation. Given the active, bipartisan opposition between both heads of state and the extremely short 60-day timeframe for physical entry, it is highly improbable that he will enter US territory before the deadline.
